# Skilo

> Share agent skills with a link. Add, sync, and pack them across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Amp, Windsurf, OpenCode, Cline, Roo, and OpenClaw.

Skilo is a transfer-first CLI and handoff layer for agent skills. Skills are directories containing a `SKILL.md` file with markdown instructions and optional YAML frontmatter. The main job is simple: share a skill, install it somewhere else, sync between tools, or make a pack. Public discovery exists, but it is secondary.

## Start Here

If you are an agent and need to decide what to do:

1. Run `npx skilo-cli --json` for the shortest machine-readable starter.
2. Use `skilo share <path|tool>` to send skills, `skilo add <source>` to install, `skilo sync <from> <to>` to copy between tools, and `skilo pack ...` to make one link from many skills.
3. Prefer `--json` on CLI commands when you need structured output.

- Human bootstrap: `npx skilo-cli` (every run refreshes the global `skilo` binary in the background)
- Agent bootstrap: `npx skilo-cli --json`
- Login: `skilo login <username>` or `skilo login --token sk_...`
- Share a skill: `skilo share ./my-skill` → returns a link
- Share a whole tool: `skilo share claude` → returns a pack when multiple skills are selected
- Share defaults to unlisted for local sources; pass `--listed` to make the skill searchable too
- Install a skill: `skilo add skilo.xyz/s/<token>` → downloads, verifies, installs
- Sync tools: `skilo sync claude opencode`
- Create a pack: `skilo pack ./reviewer namespace/design-system --name "Starter pack"` → returns a pack link
- List your published skills: `skilo list --published`

## Common Inputs

- Registry ref: `namespace/skill-name`
- Share link: `https://skilo.xyz/s/<token>` or `skilo.xyz/s/<token>`
- Pack link: `https://skilo.xyz/p/<token>`
- GitHub source: `github:user/repo` or `https://github.com/user/repo`
- Local path: `./my-skill` or `/abs/path/to/skill`

## Native Install Targets

- Claude Code: `--cc` or `--claude-code`
- Codex: `--codex`
- Cursor: `--cursor`
- Amp: `--amp`
- Windsurf: `--windsurf`
- OpenCode: `--oc` or `--opencode`
- Cline: `--cline`
- Roo: `--roo`
- OpenClaw: `--openclaw`

If no explicit target flag is passed, Skilo auto-detects installed tools. If exactly one is found, it installs there. If multiple are found, interactive runs prompt once and non-interactive runs should set `SKILO_TARGETS` or pass explicit flags. If nothing is detected, Skilo falls back to Claude Code.

## Docs

- [Documentation](https://skilo.xyz/docs.md): CLI commands, SKILL.md format, supported tools, trust model, claiming skills
- [Full context](https://skilo.xyz/llms-full.txt): All documentation in a single file
- [Changelog](https://skilo.xyz/changelog): What's new, auto-generated from commit history

## Links

- [GitHub](https://github.com/yazcaleb/skilo): Source code and issues
- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skilo-cli): CLI package
- [API root](https://skilo-api.yaz-b35.workers.dev/): machine-readable capabilities

## Optional

- [API health](https://skilo-api.yaz-b35.workers.dev/health): REST API status
- Resolve a share link: `GET https://skilo-api.yaz-b35.workers.dev/v1/skills/share/:token` → skill metadata + tarball URL
- Resolve a pack: `GET https://skilo-api.yaz-b35.workers.dev/v1/packs/:token` → pack with skill list
- Skill content is the SKILL.md file inside the tarball. The site renders it at `/s/:token`.
